name: Rajasthan
title: The assembly map Rajasthan retired in 2008
intro: For thirty years and seven elections, every Rajasthan contest was fought on the 200 constituencies of the 1976 order. The 2008 delimitation redrew all of them — and the very next election, in December 2008, was fought on the new map.
The assembly map Rajasthan retired in 2008

constituencies.name: Bhadra
constituencies.note: Retained intact (Bhadra tahsil) and shifted to the Hanumangarh district grouping with only minor boundary changes.
constituencies.extent: Bhadra tahsil.

constituencies.name: Nohar
constituencies.note: Retained largely intact and shifted to the Hanumangarh district grouping.
constituencies.extent: Nohar tahsil (excluding Rawatsar RIC).

constituencies.name: Tibi
constituencies.note: Name vanished. Tibi tahsil went to the new Sangaria; the Rawatsar belt to Pilibanga (SC); the Naurangdeshar PCs in Hanumangarh tahsil to the new Hanumangarh.
constituencies.extent: Tibi tahsil; Rawatsar RIC in Nohar tahsil; and P.Cs. 29 -Araiyawali,3i—Munda CA', 32—Munda 'B' 33—Naurangdeshar, 34 —Ranjeetpura "A", 35—Ranjeetpura "B", 36— Chohlawali, 37—Lakhuwali, "A" and 38 -Lakhuwali, "B" in Naurangdeshar RIC in Hanumangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Hanumangarh
constituencies.note: Retained with slightly revised boundaries; now grouped under Hanumangarh district.
constituencies.extent: Hanumangarh tahsil (excluding P.Cs. 29— Araiyawali, 31—Munda 'A', 32—Munda 'B', 33—Naurangdeshar, 34—Ranjeetpura 'A', 35—Ranjeetpura 'B' 36—Chohlawali, 37 -Lakhuwali 'A' and 38—Lakhuwali 'B' m Naurangdeshar RIC).

constituencies.name: Sangaria
constituencies.note: Sangaria tahsil stayed as the core of the new Sangaria; Sadulshahar tahsil was taken out to form the new Sadulshahar seat.
constituencies.extent: Sangaria and Sadulshahar tahsils.

constituencies.name: Ganganagar
constituencies.note: Retained its urban identity around Ganganagar Municipal Council and expanded by absorbing rural ILRCs from the dissolved Kesrisinghpur.
constituencies.extent: Ganganagar Municipal Council and Ganganagar (A) RIC in Ganganagar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kesrisinghpur
constituencies.note: Completely abolished; split between Sadulshahar (rural Ganganagar tahsil), Ganganagar (Burjwali & Netewala ILRCs) and Karanpur (Kesrisinghpur MB & RIC).
constituencies.extent: Ganganagar tahsil [excluding Ganganagar Municipal Council and Ganganagar (A) RIC]; Kesrisinghpur Municipal Board and Kesrisinghpur RIC in Karanpur tahsil

constituencies.name: Karanpur
constituencies.note: Reorganised to cover the whole of Karanpur and Padampur tehsils, consolidating areas earlier split across Karanpur, Kesrisinghpur and Raisinghnagar.
constituencies.extent: Karanpur tahsil (excluding Kesrisinghpur Municipal Board and Kesrisinghpur RIC) and Padampur Municipal Board, Gajsinghpur Municipal Board, Padampur and Rattewala RICs in Padampur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Raisinghnagar
constituencies.note: Kept its SC reservation and Raisinghnagar core but shed the Padampur-tahsil territory to Karanpur while gaining Vijaynagar/Anupgarh portions from old Suratgarh.
constituencies.extent: Ridmaisar and Binjhbaila RICs in Padam" pur tahsil; and Raisinghnagar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Pilibanga
constituencies.note: Territorially merged into the new Suratgarh (its area was rural Suratgarh tahsil). The Pilibanga name migrated to a different SC seat (new 9) in Hanumangarh district.
constituencies.extent: Suratgarh tahsil [excluding Suratgarh Municipal:Board, Suratgarh NMA (OG) and Suratgarh RIC].

constituencies.name: Suratgarh
constituencies.note: Split three ways: the urban core stayed in the new Suratgarh; the vast southern Anupgarh/Gharsana territory became the new Anupgarh (SC); Vijaynagar portions went to Raisinghnagar (SC).
constituencies.extent: Suratgarh Municipal Board, Suratgarh NMA (OG), and Suratgarh RIC in Suratgarh tahsil; and Anoopgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Lunkaransar
constituencies.note: Split: the core stayed as Lunkaransar while the western rural areas (Pugal, Sattasar, Jamsar belt) were transferred to the new Khajuwala (SC).
constituencies.extent: Lunkaransar tahsil; and Pugal, Sattasar and Jamsar RICs and P. Cs. 19-Napasar (including Napasar Town), 21-Tejrasar and 27-Mundsar in Bikaner RIC in Bikaner tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bikaner
constituencies.note: The single urban Bikaner seat was split into two constituencies — Bikaner West and Bikaner East.
constituencies.extent: Bikaner Municipal Council (excluding Wards 22 to 24 and 28 to 32).

constituencies.name: Kolayat
constituencies.note: Direct continuity as Kolayat (tehsil core + Deshnoke); its Bikaner Municipal Council wards returned to the urban Bikaner East/West seats.
constituencies.extent: Wards 22 to 24 and 28 to 32 in Bikaner Municipal Council. Deshnoke Municipal Board, Gangashahar Municipal Board, Bhinasar Municipal Board and Bikarer RIC [excluding P.Cs.19-Napasar (including Napasar Town), 21-Tejrasar and 27- Mundsar] in Bikaner tahsil; and Kolayat tahsil (excluding Diyatra RIC).

constituencies.name: Nokha
constituencies.note: Continued as Nokha but the SC reservation was withdrawn; the Kuchor Aathooni ILRC went to the newly created Dungargarh seat.
constituencies.extent: Diyatra RIC in Kolayat tahsil; and Nokha tahsil.

constituencies.name: Dungargarh
constituencies.note: Retained but transferred from Churu district to Bikaner district after administrative reorganisation.
constituencies.extent: Dungargarh tahsil; and Tendesar RIC, P.Cs. 16- Bidasar (including Bidasar Town) and 22-Dariba in Chhapar RIC in Sujangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sujangarh
constituencies.note: Retained as an SC seat with revised boundaries; the Chhapar area (incl. Chhapar MB) moved to Ratangarh while Bidasar MB was brought in.
constituencies.extent: Jeeli RIC. Chhapar Municipal Board, Chhapar RIC [excluding P.Cs., 16-Bidasar (including Bidasar Town) and 22-Dariba], Sujangarh Municipal Board, Sujangarh and Salasar RICs in Sujangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Ratangarh
constituencies.note: Retained; lost ILRC Golsar to Sardarshahar and gained the Chhapar area from Sujangarh for population balance.
constituencies.extent: Ratangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sardarshahar
constituencies.note: Retained; boundaries modified by transferring ILRC Ratoosar to Taranagar.
constituencies.extent: Sardarshahar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Churu
constituencies.note: The most stable seat of the district — continued to comprise Churu tehsil with only minor adjustments.
constituencies.extent: Churu tahsil.

constituencies.name: Taranagar
constituencies.note: Retained; expanded by incorporating the Dadrewa and Dokwa ILRCs from Rajgarh tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Taranagar tahsil; and Dadrewa and Dhigarla RIC in Rajgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sadulpur
constituencies.note: Continued around Rajgarh tehsil; the Dadrewa and Dhigarla ILRCs were transferred to Taranagar.
constituencies.extent: Rajgarh tahsil (excluding Dadrewa and Dhigarla RICs)

constituencies.name: Pilani
constituencies.note: Core (Pilani/Chirawa MBs) continued as Pilani, now SC-reserved; southern Sultana-RIC villages were moved into Jhunjhunu.
constituencies.extent: Chirawa RIC (excluding P.Cs. 1—Mandrela, 2—Lamba and 3—Budania), Sultana RIC (excluding P.Cs. 18—Kishorepura 19—Solana and 20—Chandana) and PCs. 9—Bangothri Kalan, 10—Likhwa, 11—Dulania and 13—Aruka in Sura; garh RIC in Chirawa tahsil.

constituencies.name: Surajgarh
constituencies.note: Core (Buhana + Surajgarh) continued as a General seat after the SC reservation was removed; Singhana fringes adjusted into Khetri.
constituencies.extent: Surajgarh RIC (excluding PCs. 9—Bangothri Kalan, 10—Likhwa, 11—Dulania and 13—Aruka) in Chirawa tahsil; Buhana and Singhana RICs in Khetri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Khetri
constituencies.note: Deep continuity around the Khetri/Babai core, absorbing minor fringes from old Surajgarh's Singhana block.
constituencies.extent: Khetri and Babai RICs in Khetri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Gudha
constituencies.note: The Gudha name disappeared; its Udaipurwati-tahsil territory continues as the new Udaipurwati seat.
constituencies.extent: Gudha Gorji RIC and Udaipur RIC (excluding P.Cs. 31-Basawa, 32-Khirod, 33-Chirana and 34-Todpura) in Udaipurwati tahsil.

constituencies.name: Nawalgarh
constituencies.note: Near-total continuity — the whole Nawalgarh tehsil preserved cleanly.
constituencies.extent: P.Cs. 31—Basawa, 32—Khirod, 33—Chirana and 34—Todpura in Udaipur RIC, Mukandgarh Municipal Board, Nawalgarh Municipal Boaid and Nawalgarh RIC in Udaipurwati tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jhunjhunu
constituencies.note: Urban core (Jhunjhunu MC + Bagar MB) retained, expanded with fringes broken off old Pilani and old Mandawa.
constituencies.extent: Jhunjhunu RIC in Jhunjhunu tahsil; and P.Cs. 1—Mandrela

constituencies.name: Mandawa
constituencies.note: Core retained as Mandawa; several eastern village circles absorbed into the expanding Jhunjhunu seat.
constituencies.extent: Mandawa and Alsisar RICs in Jhunjhunu tahsil.

constituencies.name: Fatehpur
constituencies.note: Absolute direct continuity — the entire Fatehpur tahsil boundary maintained without modification.
constituencies.extent: Fatehpur tahsil

constituencies.name: Lachhmangarh
constituencies.note: Boundary fully intact, but the SC reservation was removed, converting it into a General seat.
constituencies.extent: Lachhmangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sikar
constituencies.note: Urban core (Sikar MC + Piprali) retained; absorbed a few peripheral village circles from old Dhod.
constituencies.extent: Sikar Municipal Council, PCs. 15—Sikar Town (A ) and i6~Sikar Town (B) in Sikar RIC and Piprali RIC in Sikar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Dhod
constituencies.note: Split: the rural bulk (Dhod RIC + Losal belt) formed the new Dhod, now SC-reserved; a north-eastern fringe of PCs went to Sikar.
constituencies.extent: Sikar RICfexcluding Sikar Town Municipal Council, P.Cs. 15-Sikar Town (A) and 16—Sikar Town (B)] and Dhod RIC in Sikar tahsil; and P.Cs. n—Losal, 12—Bhima, 13—Sangliya and 14—Khood in Ramgarh RIC in Danta-Ramgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Danta - Ramgarh
constituencies.note: High continuity over Danta-Ramgarh tahsil; a few rural pockets (Bhagatpura, Jana) transferred to the new Dhod (SC).
constituencies.extent: Danta-Ramgarh tahsil (excluding P.Cs. 11—Losal, 12—Bhima, 13—Sangliya and. I4<—Khoodin Ramgarh RIC).

constituencies.name: Srimadhopur
constituencies.note: Split: town core and Ajitgarh/Mau stayed in Srimadhopur; the Reengus cluster and southern PCs were consolidated into Khandela.
constituencies.extent: Srimadhopur Municipal Board, Srimadhopur RIC (excluding P.Cs. 13-Malikpur, 14-Baori, 15-Chomu, 16-DadiyaRampura, 17-Lapwaand 24-Bassi) and Ajeetgarh RIC in Srimadhopur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Khandela
constituencies.note: Khandela core re-blocked into the new Khandela; its Neem-ka-Thana PCs (Guhala, Chala belt) returned to Neem Ka Thana.
constituencies.extent: Khandela RIC and P.Cs. 13—Malikpur, 14- Baori, 15-Chomu, 16-Dadiya Rampura, 17-Lapwa and 24-Bassi in Srimadhopur RIC in Srimadhopur tahsil ; and

constituencies.name: Neem-Ka-Thana
constituencies.note: Consolidated its core tahsil blocks (re-absorbing the Chala belt); the southern ILRC Sanwalpura Tanwaran was transferred to Srimadhopur.
constituencies.extent: Neem-ka-Thana tahsil (excluding P.Cs. i-Guhala, 2-Chala, 3-Govindpura, 12-Basdi Khurd and 13-Pithampuri in Neem-ka-Thana RIC).

constituencies.name: Chomu
constituencies.note: Core (Chomu MB, Chomu & Govindgarh RICs) retained; eastern Khejroli PCs split off to the new Shahpura seat.
constituencies.extent: Chornu Municipal Board, Chomu and Govindgarh RICs in Amber tahsil.

constituencies.name: Amber
constituencies.note: Broken up: the urban fringe became the reorganised Amber seat, while the Amber/Jairampura/Kalwad rural circles were redistributed into Jhotwara and neighbouring seats.
constituencies.extent: Amber and Jairampura RICs in Amber tahsil ; Achrol RIC in Jamwaramgarh tahsil ; and Kalwad RIC in Jaipur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jaipur Rural
constituencies.note: Name disappeared entirely. Jamdoli and Sumel went to Adarsh Nagar, the remaining rural fringe to Jhotwara, and the Jamwaramgarh RIC to Jamwa Ramgarh (ST).
constituencies.extent: Wards 7 to 11 in Jaipur Municipal Council and P.Cs. 18-Jaisinghpura Khor and 19-Jamdoli in Jaipur (Rural) RIC in Jaipur tahsil and Jamwaramgarh RIC in Jamwaramgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Hawamahal
constituencies.note: Name maintained but the ward layout inside the Municipal Corporation was completely re-blocked.
constituencies.extent: Wards 1 to 6, 17 to 20 and 22 in Jaipur Municipal Council.

constituencies.name: Johribazar
constituencies.note: Completely abolished; its walled-city wards were absorbed by the reconfigured Kishan Pole, Civil Lines and Adarsh Nagar.
constituencies.extent: Wards 21, 23 to 27 and 30 in Jaipur Municipal Council.

constituencies.name: Kishanpole
constituencies.note: Heavily altered: shed its original ward blocks and was re-blocked onto a new set of wards.
constituencies.extent: Wards 12 to 16, 31 and 33 to 35 in Jaipur Municipal Council.

constituencies.name: Bani Park
constituencies.note: Name disappeared. Its wards and rural Jaipur-tahsil territory were fragmented into Jhotwara, Vidhyadhar Nagar and Civil Lines.
constituencies.extent: Wards 28, 29, 32 and 36 to 38 in Jaipur Municipal Council and Jaipur (Rural) RIC (excluding P.Cs. 18-Jaisinghpura Khor and 19-Jamdoli) in Jaipur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Phulera
constituencies.note: Core identity around Sambhar–Phulera–Renwal kept; the Jobner belt went to Jhotwara and peripheral villages to Bagru (SC).
constituencies.extent: Renwal RIC, Jobner Municipal Board, Jobner RIC (excluding P.Cs. 20-Rojari, 21-Hirnoda, 22-Asalpur and 23 Bobas) Phulera Municipal Board, Phulera Railway Colony (OG) Sambhar Municipal Board and P.Cs. 7-Phulera,. 8-Sambhar and 10-Teonda in Sambhar RIC in Phuleratahsil.

constituencies.name: Dudu
constituencies.note: Name and SC status retained but heavily reorganised — refocused on Mauzmabad/Phagi ILRCs while its Sambhar and Jobner pieces went to Phulera and Bagru.
constituencies.extent: Sambhar RIC (excluding Sambhar Municipal Board*. PCs. 7—Phulera, 8—Sambhar and 10—Teonda) and P.Cs. 20— Rojari, 21—Hirnoda; 22—Asalpur and 23—Bobas in Jobner RIC in Phulera tahsil ; and Dudu tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sanganer
constituencies.note: Radically divided: core wards stayed in Sanganer or formed Malviya Nagar, while the vast rural layout was split off to create Bagru (SC).
constituencies.extent: Sanganer tahsil ; and Shivdaspura RIC in Chaksu tahsil.

constituencies.name: Phagi
constituencies.note: Completely dissolved. Phagi tehsil and the Chaksu core moved into the new Chaksu (SC); the Kotkhawada RIC was transferred to Bassi (ST).
constituencies.extent: Phagi tahsil; and Chaksu Municipal Board and Chaksu and Kothun RICs in Chaksu Tahsil.

constituencies.name: Lalsot
constituencies.note: Absolute continuity — the entire Lalsot tahsil with ST status intact; now grouped under Dausa district.
constituencies.extent: Lalsot Tahsil

constituencies.name: Sikrai
constituencies.note: High territorial continuity (Sikrai tahsil + Bhandarez RIC) under Dausa district, but the reservation changed from ST to SC.
constituencies.extent: Sikrai Tahsil; and Bhandarez RIC in Dausa tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bandikui
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — the Baswa tahsil footprint kept intact; now under Dausa district.
constituencies.extent: Baswa tahsil

constituencies.name: Dausa
constituencies.note: Geographically stable around Dausa town, but the SC reservation was removed.
constituencies.extent: Dausa Municipal Board and Dausa, Sainthal Alooda and Baniyana RICs in Dausa Tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bassi
constituencies.note: Core Bassi tahsil kept; became ST-reserved and absorbed Kotkhawada PCs from the dissolved Phagi.
constituencies.extent: Bassi tahsil; and Kotkhawada RIC in Chaksu tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jamwa Ramgarh
constituencies.note: Core footprint kept; became ST-reserved.
constituencies.extent: Booj, Tala, Dhola and Thali RIC Jamwa Ramarh Tahsil; and Maid RIC in Bairath tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bairath
constituencies.note: The Bairath name was retired; the entire tahsil was absorbed into the new Viratnagar seat (the standardized name of the same town).
constituencies.extent: Bairath tahsil (excluding Maid RIC).

constituencies.name: Kotputli
constituencies.note: High direct continuity over the Kotputli tahsil core.
constituencies.extent: Kotputli tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bansur
constituencies.note: Core Bansur tahsil kept; the Barod RIC returned to Behror while ILRC Narainpur was gained from Thanagazi.
constituencies.extent: Bansur tahsil; and Barod RIC in Behror tahsil

constituencies.name: Behror
constituencies.note: Core Behror area retained; the western ILRC Neemrana was split off to the new Mundawar.
constituencies.extent: Behror tahsil (excluding Barod RIC).

constituencies.name: Mandawar
constituencies.note: Continued as Mundawar (standardized spelling), expanded with ILRC Neemrana from old Behror.
constituencies.extent: Mandawar tahsil; and PCs. 29—Jonal and 31—Khanpur Ahir in Pur RIC in Kishangarh Bas tahsil

constituencies.name: Tizara
constituencies.winner_07: Amamuddin Ahamad Khan urf Durru Miyan
constituencies.note: Continued as Tijara (spelling standardized) on its tahsil core; the Kotkasim RIC was assigned to Kishangarh Bas.
constituencies.extent: Tizara tahsil; and Kotkasim RIC in Kishangarh Bas tahsil.

constituencies.name: Khairthal
constituencies.note: Name disappeared entirely. The Kishangarh Bas core became the new General seat of that name; the Chikani RIC joined the new Alwar Rural (SC).
constituencies.extent: al(SC) —Kishangarh Bas tahsil (excluding Kot Kasim RIC and PCs. 29—Jonal and 31-Khanpur Ahir in Pur RIC; and Chikani RIC in Alwar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Ramgarh
constituencies.note: Core Ramgarh tahsil preserved, with Laxmangarh-tahsil ILRCs (Govindgarh, Baroda Meo, Moliya parts) attached.
constituencies.extent: Ramgarh tahsil ; and Govindgarh RIC in Lachhmangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Alwar
constituencies.note: Bifurcated cleanly: the Municipal Council core became Alwar Urban; the rural remainder was designated Alwar Rural (SC).
constituencies.extent: Alwar Municipal Council and Alwar RIC in Alwar tahsil

constituencies.name: Thanagazi
constituencies.note: Core Thanagazi/Tehla framework kept; ILRC Narainpur was transferred to Bansur.
constituencies.extent: Thanagazi tahsil; and Tehla RIC in Rajgarh tahsil

constituencies.name: Rajgarh
constituencies.note: Reconfigured into the combined Rajgarh-Laxmangarh (ST); its Tahla/Khoh/Rajpur Bara circles went to Thanagazi.
constituencies.extent: Rajgarh tahsil (excluding Tehla RIC)

constituencies.name: Lachhmangarh
constituencies.note: Completely abolished and fragmented: western parts to Rajgarh-Laxmangarh (ST), northern circles to Ramgarh, eastern stretches to Kathumar (SC).
constituencies.extent: Umeren and Malakhera RICs in Alwar tahsil; and Ghat RIC and Lachhmangarh RIC (excluding P.Cs 25—Harsana 27—Jhalatola, 28—Khera Mangal Singh and 29—Gothra) in Lachhmangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kathumar
constituencies.note: Core identity around Kathumar kept, expanded westward with Patwar circles from the dissolved Lachhmangarh.
constituencies.extent: P.Cs. 25—Harsana, 27—Jhalatola, 28—Khera Mangal Singh and 29—Gothra in Lachhmangarh RIC, Kathumar and Sahari RICs. Kherlinganj Municipal Board and Kherlinganj RIC in Lachhmangarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kaman
constituencies.note: Core Kaman tahsil retained, realigned to integrate the newly formed Pahari tahsil area.
constituencies.extent: Kaman tahsil (excluding Gopalgarh RIC and P.Cs. 36—Sablana, 27—Birar, 28—Baroli Dhan and 29—Olanda in Sablana RIC).

constituencies.name: Nagar
constituencies.note: Core (Nagar tahsil + Gopalgarh) retained; expanded with a northern chunk of old Deeg (ILRCs Khoh, Pasta, Bedham, Januthar).
constituencies.extent: Gopalgarh RIC in Kaman tahsil ; and Nagar tahsil

constituencies.name: Deeg
constituencies.note: The standalone Deeg seat disappeared: its urban core merged with Kumher to form Deeg-Kumher, while its northern ILRCs moved into Nagar.
constituencies.extent: Deeg tahsil ; and P.Cs. 26—Sablana, 27—Birar, 28—Baroli Dhan and 29—Olanda in Sablana RIC in Kaman tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kumher
constituencies.note: Dissolved as an independent seat — the Kumher core merged with Deeg into the unified Deeg-Kumher (the name survives in the hyphen); its Bharatpur-tahsil RICs (Sewar, Dehra) went to Nadbai.
constituencies.extent: Kumher, Astawan, Sewer, Dehra and Ajan RICs and P.Cs. 1—Maghenwa, 2—Moroli Kalan, 3—Dhormui and 5—Bilothi in Jaghena RIC in Bharatpur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bharatpur
constituencies.note: Trimmed to the urban core (Bharatpur MC + Peepla/Chiksana/Jaghina); the outer rural circles (Aghapur, rural Sewar) shifted to Nadbai.
constituencies.extent: Bharatpur Municipal Board, Cimmco Factory (OG), Industrial (OG) Area, Bharatpur, Aghapur and Chiksana RICs and Jaghena RIC (excluding P.Cs. 1—Maghenwa3 2—Moroli Kalan, 3—Dhormuri and 5—Bilothi) in BharatpurB tahsil.

constituencies.name: Rupbas
constituencies.note: Name disappeared entirely. Khanuwa and Uchchain went to Nadbai; the core Rupbas/Khansurjapur/Rudawal belt merged into the new Bayana (SC).
constituencies.extent: Rupbas tahsil ; and P.Cs. 37—Mehmadpurn, 38—Palidang and 39—-Singhania in Mehmadpura RIC in Bayana tahsil

constituencies.name: Nadbai
constituencies.note: Core Nadbai tahsil kept and greatly expanded; the old Farso RIC dependency was shed to Weir (SC).
constituencies.extent: Nadbai tahsil; and Farso RIC in Bayana tahsil.

constituencies.name: Weir
constituencies.note: SC reservation and the whole Weir tahsil maintained; gained ILRCs Farso and Khareri.
constituencies.extent: Weir tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bayana
constituencies.note: Radical overhaul: became SC-reserved, absorbed the dissolved Rupbas core, and its Baseri-tahsil RICs (Ratanpur, Baseri) went to the new Baseri (SC).
constituencies.extent: Bayana tahsil (excluding Farso RIC and P.Cs. 37— Mehmadpura, 38—Palidang and 39—Singhania in Mehmadpura RIC) and Ratanpur and Baseri RICs. in Baseri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Rajakhera
constituencies.note: High continuity around Rajakhera tahsil; now under Dholpur district.
constituencies.extent: Rajakhera tahsil ;} and Mania, and Basai Nawab RICs and P.Cs. 25—Dulhara and 27—Madha Bhau in Dholpur RIC in Dholpur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Dholpur
constituencies.note: Urban core kept; ILRC Basai Nawab shifted to Bari and ILRCs Marhabhau/Maniyan to Rajakhera.
constituencies.extent: Dholpur tahsil (excluding Mania and Basai Nawab RICs. and P.Cs. 25—Dulhara and 27—Madha Bhau in Dholpur RIC).

constituencies.name: Bari
constituencies.note: Core Bari (incl. MB) kept, expanding into Saipau tehsil; its western Baseri-tehsil wing was partitioned off to form the new Baseri (SC).
constituencies.extent: Bari tahsil and Angai and Sarmathura RICs in Baser i tahsil.

constituencies.name: Karauli
constituencies.note: Substantially reorganised: Karauli town and most of the tahsil stayed in Karauli, while its Hindaun-side circles were exchanged with Hindaun (SC).
constituencies.extent: Karauli tahsil (excluding Mandrail RIC and P.Cs. 16—Gayrai 17—Lohara and 18—Atewa in Maholi RIC

constituencies.name: Sapotra
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with ST reservation; Mandrail and the Maholi circles carried into the new Sapotra.
constituencies.extent: Mandrail RIC and P.Cs. 16—Gayrai, 17— Lohara and 18—Atewa in Maholi RIC in Karauli tahsil ; and Sapotra tahsil.

constituencies.name: Khandar
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; absorbed the Chauth Ka Barwara and Phalaudi areas of old Sawai Madhopur to become more compact.
constituencies.extent: Khandar tahsil; and Phalodi RIC, P.Cs. 11— Bhagwat Garh, 15—Sunari and 17—Jonla in Surwal RIC, P.Cs. 32—Banjari 33—Adalwara Kalan, 34—Ramdi, 39— Ghamira and 41—Dhekwa in Jatwara Khurd RIC and Chothka-Barwara RIC in Sawai Madhopur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sawai Madhopur
constituencies.note: Significantly reduced: the municipal core, Surwal and Malarna Dungar stayed; western areas shifted to Khandar (SC) and the Bonli-side pockets to Bamanwas (ST).
constituencies.extent: Chakeri RIC, Surwal RIC (excluding P.Cs. 11—Bhagwat-Garh, 15—Sunari and 17—Jonla), Sawai Madhopur Municipal Board, Sawai Madhopur Spl. Charge Cement Factory (NMA), Man Town (N.A.), Jatwara Khurd RIC (excluding P.Cs. 32—Banjari, 33—Adalwara Kalan 34— Ramdi, 39—Ghamira and 41—Dhekwa) in Sawai Madhopur tahsil; and Malarna Dungar RIC, P.Cs. 17—Hathdoli, 19—Hindupura and 20—Badagaon in Peepalwara RIC and P.Cs. 23—Khirni, 24—Jolanda and 25—Bharoti in Malarna Chour KIC in Bonli tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bamanwas
constituencies.note: High continuity — the Bamanwas and Bonli belt continued as the new Bamanwas (ST) constituency.
constituencies.extent: Bonli RIC, Peepalwara RIC (excluding P.Cs. 17—Hathdoli, 19—Hindupura and 20—Badagaon) and Malarna Chour RIC (excluding P.Cs. 23—Khirni, 24— Jolanda and 25—Bharoti) in Bonli tahsil ; and Bamanwas tahsil

constituencies.name: Gangapur
constituencies.note: Almost entirely unchanged — Gangapur tahsil throughout.
constituencies.extent: Gangapur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Hindaun
constituencies.note: Name continued; parts of Hindaun tehsil were ceded to the reconstituted Karauli.
constituencies.extent: Hindaun Tahsil

constituencies.name: Mahuwa
constituencies.note: Continued but shifted to the Dausa district grouping; expanded into Baswa tehsil while its Mundia RIC went to Todabhim.
constituencies.extent: Mahuwa tahsil; and Mundia RIC in Toda Bhim tahsil.

constituencies.name: Toda Bhim
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with standardised spelling — 'Toda Bhim' became 'Todabhim' — with ILRC-level boundary revisions.
constituencies.extent: Nadoti tahsil; and Toda Bhim tahsil (excluding Mundia RIC).

constituencies.name: Niwai
constituencies.note: Reserved status kept; now covers the whole Niwai plus Peeplu tehsils, returning the Sohela circle to Tonk.
constituencies.extent: Niwai tahsil ; and Sohela RIC in Tonk tahsil.

constituencies.name: Tonk
constituencies.note: Tonk town core retained; Sohela and Jhirana removed earlier, and parts of Todaraisingh tehsil added.
constituencies.extent: Tonk tahsil (excluding Sohela and Jhirana RICs).

constituencies.name: Uniara
constituencies.note: Merged with Deoli tehsil to create the new Deoli-Uniara; the standalone 'Uniara' name disappeared.
constituencies.extent: Uniara tahsil; and Dooni and Ghad RICs in Deoli tahsil.

constituencies.name: Todaraisingh
constituencies.note: Ceased to exist: the Todaraisingh municipal core shifted to Malpura, while the Khareda and Lamba Kalan areas became part of Tonk.
constituencies.extent: Todaraisingh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Malpura
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with expansion — absorbed most of Todaraisingh tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Malpura tahsil; and Jhirana RIC in Tonk tahsil. Key Chances

constituencies.name: Kishangarh
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; expanded with the Akodia area and parts of Sarwar tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Kishangarh tahsil (excluding Akodia RIC).

constituencies.name: Ajmer East
constituencies.note: Abolished: the reserved urban wards became Ajmer South (SC), northern wards went to Ajmer North, and the rural west joined the new Pushkar.
constituencies.extent: Wards 1, 8 to 13, 16 and 27 to 36 in Ajmer Municipal Council and Ajmer Cantonment

constituencies.name: Ajmer West
constituencies.note: Disappeared — its municipal wards were reorganised into the newly created Ajmer North and Ajmer South (SC).
constituencies.extent: Ajmer Municipal Council (excluding Wards 1 8 to 13, 16 and 27 to 36).

constituencies.name: Pushkar
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with major expansion — now takes in rural Ajmer tehsil and parts of Kishangarh tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Gagwana RIC, Subhashnagar (OG), Regional College (OG), Ajmer-I RIC (excluding Ajmer Municipal Council) Pushkar Municipal Board, Pushkar RIC, Saradhana RIC, and Pisangan RIC in Ajmer tahsil.

constituencies.name: Nasirabad
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; now consists mainly of the Nasirabad and Pisangan tehsils instead of scattered RICs.
constituencies.extent: Ajmer-II, Shrinagar and Ramsar RICs, Nasirabad Town (CA), Derathu and Mangaliyawas RICs in Ajmer tahsil.

constituencies.name: Beawar
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with boundary rationalisation — centred almost entirely on Beawar tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Beawar Municipal Council, P.C. 63—Naya Nagar in Naya Nagar RIC, Khera Kalan and Jawaja RICs and Rajiawas RIC (excluding P.Cs. 75—Atitmand, 76—Gohana, 81—Narbad Kheda, 82—Malpura and 86—Shahpura Kalinjar) in Beawar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Masuda
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; expanded to the whole Masuda tehsil plus Bhinay tehsil.
constituencies.extent: P.Cs. 75—Atitmand,

constituencies.name: Bhinai
constituencies.note: Disappeared completely: most of its territory (Bhinay, Sarwar) became part of the new Kekri, while Akodia shifted to Kishangarh.
constituencies.extent: Deolia Kalan, Bhinai and Bandanwara RICs in Kekri tahsil; Sarwar tahsil (excluding Sarwar Municipal Board and Sarwar RIC); and Akodia RIC in Kishangarh tahsil

constituencies.name: Kekri
constituencies.note: Name retained but completely reorganised — the SC reservation was dropped and it absorbed most of the former Bhinai plus Sarwar tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Sarwar Municipal Board and Sarwar RIC in Sarwar tahsil; and Kekri tahsil (excluding Deolia Kalan RIC, Bhinai RIC and Bandanwara RIC).

constituencies.name: Hindoli
constituencies.note: Hindoli tahsil retained as the core; the new seat also gained the Nainwa town side (Nainwa MB, Jajawar, Bansi, Dei) of old Nainwa.
constituencies.extent: Hindoli tahsil; and P.Cs. 5—Silore, 6—Ramnagar 7—Gudha, 8—Mangal and 9—Neem-Ka-Khera in Bundf RIC in Bundi tahsil.

constituencies.name: Nainwa
constituencies.note: Name discontinued and genuinely split in two: Jetpur/Karwar/Bamangaon went to Keshoraipatan (SC); the Nainwa town belt to Hindoli.
constituencies.extent: Nainwa tahsil; and Khatkar RIC in Bundi tahsil.

constituencies.name: Patan
constituencies.note: Name partially continues — Patan became Keshoraipatan (SC), with the old Keshorai Patan tahsil as its core and the SC reservation retained.
constituencies.extent: Keshorai Patan tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bandi
constituencies.note: Direct continuity by spelling variant — 'Bandi' became the cleanly tahsil-shaped Bundi seat.
constituencies.extent: Bundi tahsil (excluding Khatkar RIC and P.Cs. 5—Silore, 6—Ramnagar, 7—Gudha, 8—Mangal and 9—NeemKa-Khera i n Bundi RIC).

constituencies.name: Kota
constituencies.note: The urban Kota seat was split into two: northern municipal wards form Kota North, southern wards Kota South.
constituencies.extent: Kota Municipal Council (excluding Wards 1 to 3 and 30 to 33).

constituencies.name: Ladpura
constituencies.note: Name retained but the Kota city core was lost to the new urban seats; now mainly rural Ladpura and Kaithoon.
constituencies.extent: Wards 1 to 3 and 30 to 33 in Kota Municipal Council, Ladpura and Kaithoon RICs in Ladpura tahsil.

constituencies.name: Digod
constituencies.note: Abolished — Digod tehsil was divided between the newly created Pipalda and Sangod constituencies.
constituencies.extent: Digod tahsil and Sangod tahsil (excluding Bapawar RIC) of Kotah district; and PC Gagron in Jhalawar RIC in Jhalrapatan tahsil of Jhalawar district.

constituencies.name: Pipalda
constituencies.note: Continues under the same name with a much larger area after absorbing most of old Digod; the SC reservation was removed.
constituencies.extent: Pipalda tahsil ; and Mangrol RIC (excluding P.Cs. 5—Jarela, 6—Bohat and io—Isharpura) in Mangrol tahsil.

constituencies.name: Baran
constituencies.note: Moved out of Kota district: continued as the renamed Anta seat in the new Baran district, absorbing all of Mangrol tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Mangrol tahsil (excluding Mangrol RIC) and Baran RIC in Baran tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kishanganj
constituencies.note: The core Kishanganj–Shahbad territory continued as Kishanganj (ST) in Baran district; its Mangrol-RIC PCs merged into the new Anta.
constituencies.extent: P.Cs. 5—Jarela, 6—Bohat and 10—Isharura in Mangrol RIC in Mangrol tahsil; Kishanganj and Shahbad tahsils

constituencies.name: Atru
constituencies.note: Merged with the Baran town area to form the new Baran-Atru (SC) seat in Baran district, keeping its SC reservation.
constituencies.extent: Kalmanda RIC in Baran tahsil; Bapawar RIC in Sangod tahsil; and Atru tahsil

constituencies.name: Chhabra
constituencies.note: The only completely unchanged seat — same Chhabra and Chhipabarod tehsils, now under Baran district.
constituencies.extent: Chhabra and Chipabarod tahsils.

constituencies.name: Ramganjmandi
constituencies.note: Retained but became SC-reserved, expanding with rural Ladpura's Mandana circles.
constituencies.extent: Ramganjmandi tahsil ; and Mandana RIC in Ladpura Tahsil.

constituencies.name: Khanpur
constituencies.note: Name kept; expanded into Jhalrapatan tehsil while its Aklera portion shifted to Manohar Thana.
constituencies.extent: Khanpur tahsil; and Aklera RIC (excluding P.Cs7—Lahas and 8—Dewari Kalan) in Aklera tahsil.

constituencies.name: Manohar Thana
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with significant expansion — absorbed the entire Aklera tehsil and parts of Jhalrapatan.
constituencies.extent: P.Cs. 7—Lahas and 8—Dewari Kalan in Aklera RIC, Ghatoli Bhalta, Manohar Thana, Jawar and Banskheri RICs in Aklera tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jhalrapatan
constituencies.note: Urban core around Jhalrapatan town retained; several rural ILRCs moved to the reorganised Khanpur and Manohar Thana.
constituencies.extent: Jhalrapatan tahsil (excluding PC. Gagron in Jhalawar RIC).

constituencies.name: Pirawa
constituencies.note: Abolished — Pirawa tehsil became the core of the expanded Jhalrapatan, while the Ganeshpura area shifted to Dag (SC).
constituencies.extent: Pirawa tahsil; and Ganeshpura RIC in Pachpahar tahsil

constituencies.name: Dag
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with SC reservation retained; gained the Ganeshpura area from the former Pirawa.
constituencies.extent: Gangdhar tahsil; and Pachpahar tahsil (excluding Ganeshpura RIC).

constituencies.name: Begun
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; expanded considerably by incorporating Rawatbhata tehsil and most of Gangrar.
constituencies.extent: Begun tahsil.

constituencies.name: Gangrar
constituencies.note: Abolished: most of Gangrar tehsil merged with Begun, while the Chittorgarh MB area joined the new Chittorgarh seat.
constituencies.extent: Gangrar and Rashmi tahsils.

constituencies.name: Kapasin
constituencies.note: Continued but became SC-reserved; expanded with Rashmi tehsil and parts of Bhadesar.
constituencies.extent: Kapasin tahsil; and Bhadsora RIC in Bhadesar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Chittorgarh
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; retained the town and expanded into parts of Gangrar and Bhadesar tehsils.
constituencies.extent: Chittorgarh tahsil; and Bhadesar RIC in Bhadesar tahsil

constituencies.name: Nimbahera
constituencies.note: Continued centred on Nimbahera and Chhoti Sadri tehsils.
constituencies.extent: Nimbahera and Chhoti Sadri tahsils

constituencies.name: Badi Sadri
constituencies.note: Continued after a name change (Badi Sadri → Bari Sadri), expanded with Dungla tehsil and parts of Bhadesar and Pratapgarh.
constituencies.extent: Dungla and Badi Sadri tahsils; and Dhamotar RIC in Partabgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Pratapgarh
constituencies.note: Continued with standardized spelling (Partapgarh → Pratapgarh); centred on the newly formed Pratapgarh district with ST reservation retained.
constituencies.extent: Partapgarh (ST) —Partabgarh tahsil (excluding Dhamotar RIC).

constituencies.name: Kushalgarh
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — Kushalgarh tehsil with ST reservation unchanged.
constituencies.extent: Kushalgarh tahsil.

constituencies.name: Danpur
constituencies.note: Abolished; its territory was divided mainly between the newly created Garhi (ST), the reorganised Banswara (ST) and Bagidora (ST).
constituencies.extent: Danpur RIC and Banswara RIC (excluding P.Cs. 4—Kuwala, 5—Kupda, 6—Galkia and 8 —Banswara and Banswara Municipal Board) in Banswara tahsil; and PCs. 1—Bodigama, 3—Chhinch, 4—Chokhala, 5—Badodiya, 6—Kalinjara and 7—Karaji in Bagidora RIC in Bagidora tahsil.

constituencies.name: Ghatol
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — centred on Ghatol tehsil with only minor adjustments.
constituencies.extent: Ghatol tahsil.

constituencies.name: Banswara
constituencies.note: Continued but became ST-reserved; the Talwara area was transferred to the new Garhi (ST).
constituencies.extent: Banswara Municipal Board, P.Cs. 4—Kuwala, 5—Kupda, 6—Galkia and 8—Banswara in Banswara RIC in Banswara tahsil; Metwala RIC and P.Cs. 19—Daduka, 20— Partapur, 21—Garhi, 22—Bori and 24—Ajna in Garhi RIC in Garhi tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bagidora
constituencies.note: Name retained but more compact after transferring the Garhi-tahsil area to the newly created Garhi (ST).
constituencies.extent: Garhi tahsil (excluding Metwala RIC and P.Cs. 19—Daduka, 20—Partapur, 21—Garhi, 22—Bori and 24—Ajna in Garhi RIC); and Bagidora tahsil (excluding P.Cs 1—Bodigama 3—Chhinch, 4—Chokhala, 5—Badodiya" 6—Kalinjara and 7—Karaji in Bagidora RIC).

constituencies.name: Sagwara
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — Sagwara tehsil remains almost entirely intact.
constituencies.extent: Sagwara tahsil (excluding Padwa RIC).

constituencies.name: Chorasi
constituencies.note: Partially reorganised: Bichhiwara and Genji areas shifted into Dungarpur (ST); the Simalwara region forms the new Chorasi (ST).
constituencies.extent: Chorasi and Genji RICs and P.Cs. 24—Amjhara 25—Kanba 26—Bichhiwara 27—Modar and 28—Chundawara in Bichhiwara RIC in Dungarpur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Dungarpur
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — Dungarpur town remains the core with village-level adjustments.
constituencies.extent: Dungarpur tahsil (excluding Chorasi RIC, Genji RIC and P. Cs. 24—Amjhara 25—Kanba 26—Bichhiwara, 27—Modar, and 28—Chundawara in Bichhiwara RIC).

constituencies.name: Aspur
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; only the Padwa RIC was transferred to Sagwara (ST).
constituencies.extent: Aspur tahsil ; and Padwa RIC in Sagwara tahsil.

constituencies.name: Lasadia
constituencies.note: Name disappeared. The Lasadia–Bhabrana tribal belt was absorbed into the newly created Dhariawad (ST) with adjustments alongside Salumber (ST).
constituencies.extent: Lasadia tahsil; and Bhabrana RIC in Salumber tahsil.

constituencies.name: Vallabhnagar
constituencies.note: Direct continuity; a few Girwa-tehsil villages added for population balance.
constituencies.extent: Vallabhnagar tahsil.

constituencies.name: Mavli
constituencies.note: Mavli core retained, expanded into parts of Girwa; its Relmagra-side circles fell to the new Rajsamand district.
constituencies.extent: Mavli tahsil; and RIC No. 2 (excluding P. Cs. 11—Khandel 12—Junda and 13—Bamniya Kalan) in Relmagra tahsil.

constituencies.name: Rajsamand
constituencies.note: Moved out of Udaipur district entirely — the core became the new Rajsamand seat in the newly created Rajsamand district, as a General seat.
constituencies.extent: Rajsamand tahsil; and RIC No. 1 and P.Cs. 11—Khandel, 12—Junda and 13—Bamniya Kalan in RIC No. 2 in Relmagra tahsil.

constituencies.name: Nathdwara
constituencies.note: Continued as Nathdwara under the new Rajsamand district.
constituencies.extent: Nathdwara tahsil.

constituencies.name: Udaipur
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with urban reorganisation — redrawn to selected municipal wards after Udaipur Rural (ST) was formed.
constituencies.extent: Wards 1 to 20, 22 to 27 and 32 to 34 in Udaipur Municipal Council.

constituencies.name: Udaipur Rural
constituencies.note: Continued as an ST seat with major revision — gained extensive rural Girwa/Debari areas while inner wards shifted to Udaipur city.
constituencies.extent: Udaipur Municipal Council (excluding Wards 1 to 20, 22 to 27 and 32 to 34), Udaipur City RIC, Debari RIC and Barapal RIC (excluding P. Cs. 25—Tidi, 26—Paduna and 27—Chanawada) in Girwa tahsil.

constituencies.name: Salumber
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with additions from Sarada tehsil and minor Girwa-boundary adjustments.
constituencies.extent: Salumber tahsil (excluding Bhabrana RIC); and Kurawad RIC in Girwa tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sarada
constituencies.note: Abolished — the Sarada name disappeared as its territory was divided between Salumber (ST) and the newly created Dhariawad (ST).
constituencies.extent: Sarada tahsil; and P.Cs. 25—Tidi 26—Paduna and 27—Chanawada in Barapal RIC in Girwa tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kherwara
constituencies.note: Direct continuity — largely unchanged with minor boundary adjustments.
constituencies.extent: Kherwara tahsil.

constituencies.name: Phalasia
constituencies.note: Renamed and reorganised as Jhadol (ST), incorporating the Kotra tribal areas while keeping much of the original core.
constituencies.extent: Phalasia tahsil; and Kotra tahsil (excluding Mairpur RIC and P.Cs. 3—Mandwa, 8—Beekarni, 13—Samoli and 14—Jhaid in Kotra RIC).

constituencies.name: Gongunda
constituencies.note: Retained its name and expanded — absorbed the Kotra fringe and Girwa-tehsil circles.
constituencies.extent: Gogunda (ST) —Gogunda tahsil; and Mairpur RIC, and P.Cs. 3—Mandwa, 8—Beekarni, 13—Samoli and 14—Jhaid in Kotra RIC in Kotra tahsil.

constituencies.name: Kumbhalgarh
constituencies.note: Removed from Udaipur district — became part of the new Rajsamand district configuration.
constituencies.extent: Kumbhalgarh and Amet tahsils.

constituencies.name: Bhim
constituencies.note: Removed from Udaipur district — continued as Bhim under Rajsamand district.
constituencies.extent: Bhim and Deogarh tahsils.

constituencies.name: Mandal
constituencies.note: Retained; expanded into rural Bhilwara-tahsil ILRCs while the Pur belt was redistributed.
constituencies.extent: Mandal tahsil ; and Pur RIC in Bhilwara tahsil

constituencies.name: Sahada
constituencies.note: Continued with almost the same territorial base but officially renamed from Sahada to Sahara.
constituencies.extent: Sahada and Raipur tahsils.

constituencies.name: Bhilwara
constituencies.note: Reduced to the Bhilwara Municipal Council area; the surrounding rural areas were transferred to Mandal and Sahara.
constituencies.extent: Bhilwara tahsil (excluding Pur RIC).

constituencies.name: Mandalgarh
constituencies.note: Direct continuity, with Bijoliya and parts of Kotri tehsil added.
constituencies.extent: Mandalgarh tahsil; and Badlias RIC in Kotri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jahazpur
constituencies.note: Direct continuity with minor additions from Kotri tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Jahazpur tahsil; and Paroli RIC in Kotri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Shahpura
constituencies.note: Continued as the district's SC seat, with Banera tehsil added and Kotri areas removed.
constituencies.extent: Shahpura tahsil; and Kotri RIC in Kotri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Banera
constituencies.note: Abolished — the Banera name disappeared; the tehsil was divided mainly between the enlarged Asind and Shahpura (SC).
constituencies.extent: Banera and Hurda tahsils.

constituencies.name: Asind
constituencies.note: Expanded substantially — retained its name and absorbed the former Hurda and Banera areas.
constituencies.extent: Asind tahsil.

constituencies.name: Jaitaran
constituencies.note: Retained with modified boundaries — expanded into Raipur tehsil (Kot Kirana, Babra, Sendra).
constituencies.extent: Jaitaran tahsil ; and P. Cs. 18—Babra and 19—Surnei in Sendra RIC in Raipur tahsil.

constituencies.name: Raipur
constituencies.note: Abolished — its Raipur-tehsil territory was merged into Jaitaran and Sojat (SC).
constituencies.extent: Raipur tahsil (excluding PCs. 18—Babra and 19—Sumel in Sendra RIC) and Bagri RIC (excluding PCs. 21—Khokra, 22—Bagri 23—Hariyamali 28—Gura—Kalan and 32—Siyat) in Sojat tahsil.

constituencies.name: Sojat
constituencies.note: Retained but converted from General to SC-reserved, consolidated on Sojat tehsil.
constituencies.extent: Sojat Municipal Board, Sojat and Sheopura RICs and P. Cs- 21—Khokra 22—Bagri 23—Hariyamali 28—Gurakalan and 32—Siyat in Bagri RIC in Sojat tahsil; and Manda RIC (excluding P. Cs. 17—Saran and 20—Siriyari) in Kharchi tahsil

constituencies.name: Kharchi
constituencies.note: Abolished — the Kharchi name disappeared; its territory was merged into the new Marwar Junction and Bali.
constituencies.extent: Kharchi and Ranawas RICs. and PCs. 17—Saran and 20—Siriyari in Manda RIC in Kharchi tahsil; and Magartalao RIC in Desuri tahsil.

constituencies.name: Desuri
constituencies.note: Abolished — Desuri was merged into Bali (Sadri/Nadol side) with the Magartalao circle going to Marwar Junction.
constituencies.extent: Desuri tahsil (excluding Magartalao RIC) and Khod

constituencies.name: Pali
constituencies.note: Retained as a compact seat (Pali Municipal Council + Rohat); its rural tahsil areas passed to Sumerpur.
constituencies.extent: Pali tahsil (excluding Gundoj and Khod RICs.)

constituencies.name: Sumerpur
constituencies.note: Retained around Sumerpur; its Bali-tahsil RICs (Sanderao, Takhatgarh) returned to Bali.
constituencies.extent: Gundoj RIC in Pali tahsil; and Sanderao and Takhatgarh RICs. and Sumerpur RIC (excluding P. Cs 22—Bisalpur and 23—Perivakalan) in Bali tahsil.

constituencies.name: Bali
